title: STANDARD FOR FORMULA FOODS FOR USE IN VERY LOW ENERGY DIETS FOR WEIGHT REDUCTION

sections.section: STANDARD FOR FORMULA FOODS FOR USE IN VERY LOW ENERGY DIETS FOR WEIGHT REDUCTION
sections.summary: Document title only: Standard for formula foods intended for use in very low energy diets for weight reduction.
Heading only: identifies the Codex standard CXS 203-1995 and contains no substantive provisions.
Adoption note: the standard was adopted in 1995 and amended in 2023.
2023 amendments: decisions at the 46th Codex Alimentarius Commission moved food‑additive provisions into the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192‑1995) and amended Section 7 (Packaging).

Scope: applies to formula foods for very low energy diets used as foods for special medical purposes for weight reduction, to be used under medical supervision for persons with moderate or severe obesity, with prescription sales left to national decision; excludes pre‑packaged conventional meals.

Definition: a formula food for very low energy diets is a specially prepared food providing the daily requirements of essential nutrients and a minimum amount of carbohydrates within 450–800 kcal as the sole source of energy.

Essential composition and quality factors: the product as sold must meet specified composition and quality requirements detailed in subsections covering energy, nutrients (protein, fats, carbohydrates, vitamins/minerals) and ingredients.
Energy content: when prepared according to instructions the formula shall provide a daily energy intake of 450–800 kcal as the only source of energy.

Nutrient contents (overview): the recommended daily intake must meet specific minimums and limits including protein (see 3.2.1), fats (see 3.2.2), carbohydrates (see 3.2.3) and supply 100% of recommended daily intakes for vitamins and minerals (see 3.2.4), with specific amounts listed for several vitamins and calcium.

Protein: the recommended daily intake shall contain at least 50 g protein with a protein‑digestibility‑corrected amino acid score of 1; essential amino acids may be added only as necessary and only L‑forms are permitted except DL‑methionine.
Fats: the recommended daily intake shall provide at least 3 g linoleic acid and less than 0.5 g α‑linolenic acid with a linoleic/α‑linolenic ratio between 5 and 15.
Carbohydrates: the recommended daily intake shall provide not less than 50 g of available carbohydrates.

Vitamins and minerals: the product shall provide 100% of recommended daily intakes for vitamins and minerals, with specified amounts listed (e.g., Vitamin A 600 µg, Vitamin D 2.5 µg, Vitamin E 10 mg, Vitamin C 30 mg, folic acid 200 µg, calcium 500 mg, etc.).

Ingredients: formulae shall be prepared from animal and/or plant protein constituents proven suitable for human consumption and other suitable ingredients necessary to meet the composition requirements of Sections 3.1 and 3.2.
Food additives: only additives permitted in Table 1 and Table 2 of the GSFA (CXS 192‑1995) for food category 13.4 or those listed in Table 3 are acceptable for foods conforming to this standard.

Contaminants (overview): products must be manufactured to minimize pesticide residues in line with Codex MRLs and be free of hormones and antibiotics (and practically free of other pharmacologically active substances), determined by agreed analytical methods.

Pesticide residues: products shall be produced under good manufacturing practices to prevent pesticide residues or, if unavoidable, reduce them to the maximum extent possible and comply with Codex maximum residue limits for the commodity.

Other contaminants: the product shall be free from residues of hormones and antibiotics as determined by agreed methods of analysis and practically free from other contaminants, especially pharmacologically active substances.

Hygiene: under good manufacturing practices the product shall be free from objectionable matter and, when tested, shall be free from pathogenic microorganisms, microbial‑origin substances at hazardous levels, and any other poisonous or deleterious substances at hazardous levels.

Packaging: products must be packed in containers that safeguard hygienic qualities, liquids must be thermally processed and hermetically sealed to ensure sterility, and packaging materials must be safe and suitable (subject to any applicable Codex packaging standards); the section also includes a table of mineral amounts (e.g., phosphorus 500 mg, iron 16 mg, iodine 140 µg, magnesium 350 mg, zinc 6 mg, potassium 1.6 g, sodium 1 g).

Fill of container: for ready‑to‑eat products the fill shall be at least 80% v/v for containers <150 g, at least 85% v/v for 150–250 g, and at least 90% v/v for >250 g, where water capacity is defined as the volume of distilled water at 20 °C a sealed container holds when completely filled.

Sets labelling requirements for "Formula food for use in very low energy diets", including mandatory name (Section 9.1), a full ingredients list per the General Standard (Section 9.2), declaration of nutritive value per 100 g/100 ml (energy, protein, available carbohydrates, fat, specified vitamins/minerals and optional other nutrients) and optional expression as percentages of recommended daily standards (Section 9.3), date marking per the General Standard (Section 9.4), storage instructions for unopened and opened products including warnings if not storable after opening (Section 9.5), and required utilisation statements—including "for the dietary management of obesity" near the name, advice on fluid intake, and warnings that the product should not be used by pregnant/nursing women, infants, children, adolescents and elderly except when medically indicated—with provision for some information to appear on an accompanying leaflet provided the label refers to it (Sections 9.6–9.7).

Requires a statement that the product may not be recommended for uses other than dietary management of obesity, mandates that the name and indications (Sections 9.1 and 9.6) appear on the consumer package/sachet label, and allows other required statements to appear on an accompanying leaflet provided the label references the leaflet.
